b' The DeSantis Revival Plot & McCarthy\xe2\x80\x99s Vendetta
Happy Thursday. Welcome to The Best & The Brightest. Tonight, I look at how Ron DeSantis is reemerging from his shell post-Iowa and plotting his way toward 2028\xe2\x80\x94starting with making nice with Donald Trump. DeSantis, ignoring the lessons of 2024, is returning to his familiar playbook of running to the far right, with plans to spend tens of millions of dollars to preserve his six-week abortion ban and endear himself to evangelicals in Iowa. \xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c

Happy Thursday. Welcome to The Best & The Brightest. I\xe2\x80\x99m Tara Palmeri .

Tonight, I look at how Ron DeSantis is reemerging from his shell post-Iowa and plotting his way toward 2028\xe2\x80\x94starting with making nice with Donald Trump . DeSantis, ignoring the lessons of 2024, is returning to his familiar playbook of running to the far right, with plans to spend tens of millions of dollars to preserve his six-week abortion ban and endear himself to evangelicals in Iowa.

\xf0\x9f\x8e\xa7 Also, don\xe2\x80\x99t miss the latest episode of Somebody\xe2\x80\x99s Gotta Win , featuring Puck\xe2\x80\x99s very own John Heilemann , the latest addition to the Best & Brightest team. We discuss Michael Cohen \xe2\x80\x99s testimony in the Trump hush money trial and the impact the verdict could have on voters in November. Plus the state of the Trump and Biden campaigns, and whether the upcoming CNN and ABC News presidential debates will move the needle. Listen here or here . I lost my voice, but as soon as it comes back, I will post another episode for the holiday weekend.

Let\xe2\x80\x99s get started. First up, Abby Livingston \xe2\x80\x99s readout on some upcoming primary races\xe2\x80\xa6

Burning Down the House
House members are on a hot streak in the primaries. Only one federal incumbent has lost this cycle: Alabama\xe2\x80\x99s Jerry Carl , who fell to fellow congressman Barry Moore after the courts redrew their respective districts, leading to the member vs. member showdown. But beginning Tuesday and throughout the summer, more than a dozen House incumbents will face serious primary challenges. (Senators, for their part, seem to be in the clear.)

On the Republican side, the battle lines have mostly been drawn around who did, and did not, support Kevin McCarthy when he was ousted from the speakership. On the Democratic side, the dividing line is all about Israel. This rundown is based on television ad spending, candidate fundraising, outside group spending, and of course, plenty of inside chatter.

  • McCarthy\xe2\x80\x99s vendetta : McCarthy is out for blood. The super PAC run by his longtime lieutenant, Brian O. Walsh , is spending big against two Republican members who voted to oust him: South Carolina\xe2\x80\x99s Nancy Mace and Virginia\xe2\x80\x99s Bob Good . Both Mace and Good, however, are getting help from conservative groups. The consensus is that Mace is probably in better shape than Good; his primary will take place on June 18, and Mace\xe2\x80\x99s on June 8. Two other anti-McCarthy incumbents are also in the barrel: Arizona\xe2\x80\x99s Eli Crane and Florida\xe2\x80\x99s Matt Gaetz , although those late-summer primaries haven\xe2\x80\x99t developed enough to draw any firm conclusions.

  • Moderates under fire : Right-wing groups are also on the hunt to take out some incumbents this cycle. The biggest race is on Tuesday, when second-term Texan Tony Gonzales faces conservative rival Brandon Herrera . Gonzales is an incredibly savvy campaigner, and he and his allies have outspent the opposition by millions . Even so, a number of G.O.P. strategists are nervous.

    Meanwhile, newly installed House Approps chairman Tom Cole is running as if his career depends on it. The longtime Oklahoma congressman and his super PAC allies are spending big on his June 18 primary, even though most strategists think he\xe2\x80\x99ll be fine. There are some frustrations surrounding the Approps chair having to spend money on his own reelection rather than fundraising for the N.R.C.C. Other incumbents to watch: Utah\xe2\x80\x99s Celeste Maloy (June 25), South Carolina\xe2\x80\x99s William Timmons (June 20), Washington State\xe2\x80\x99s Dan Newhouse , and Florida\xe2\x80\x99s Dan Webster (both on August 20).

  • The anti-Israel wave : A handful of Squad members are facing challenges from the pro-Israel side of the Democratic Party. Jamaal Bowman \xe2\x80\x99s reelection bid in New York is the talk of the political class: He\xe2\x80\x99s running a well-funded campaign, but AIPAC-supported rival George Latimer has outraised him and has serious support coming in from outside groups.

    Meanwhile, Missouri\xe2\x80\x99s Cori Bush has fundraised at a similar clip as Wesley Bell , her Democratic opponent, but her burn rate has given Bell a meaningful cash-on-hand advantage in the August 6 primary. In Minnesota, Ilhan Omar is again facing Don Samuels , who nearly toppled her two years ago, but the three-term congresswoman recently won the state party\xe2\x80\x99s endorsement and has only expanded her already strong fundraising machine.

  • The outliers : And then there are a pair of races that don\xe2\x80\x99t fit neatly into larger political trends. Louisiana Republican Garret Graves drew the short end of the stick during Louisiana\xe2\x80\x99s redistricting, and his fellow Louisianan Mike Johnson subtly signaled his preference for Julia Letlow, should Graves run against her in the 5th district. Then New Jersey Democrat Rob Menendez faces Ravi Bhalla on June 4, in a rare instance when a family dynasty association backfires: His father, New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez , is currently on trial for political corruption.
The Ron Revival
After the Shell Bay Club d\xc3\xa9tente, Ronny is out there shilling for his old nemesis, Trump is dangling convention spots, and the DeSantis brain trust is eyeing a path to \xe2\x80\x9928.
TARA PALMERI
Last month, at the Shell Bay Club in Miami, Ron DeSantis finally made amends with Donald Trump . The meeting, brokered by their mutual donor and New York real estate tycoon Steve Witkoff , was reciprocally beneficial and grudgingly accepted. Trump, according to my sources, was told that the d\xc3\xa9tente was required for Witkoff to drop even more money into his campaign. He was also assured that the Florida governor would commit to raising money for him.

DeSantis, who insisted on a neutral site, was after something even more valuable: a relationship with the potential future president, a reputational reboot within his party, and the possibility of a primetime speaking slot at the convention\xe2\x80\x94itself a way to paper over the ignominy of a disastrous year. \xe2\x80\x9cWitkoff was like, \xe2\x80\x98You have to make nice with Ron,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d said a source close to the donor. \xe2\x80\x9cWitkoff believes DeSantis is the future, and [DeSantis] knows he needs Trump\xe2\x80\x99s endorsement in his future. There\xe2\x80\x99s a repair job and it\xe2\x80\x99s donor-driven. The donors are playing the long game.\xe2\x80\x9d

DeSantis, it appears, is playing the long game, too. After issuing a hostage statement Trump endorsement in January, DeSantis went into hibernation long enough to ensure that the former president got his various nickname jabs out of his system. Now he\xe2\x80\x99s re-emerged, leaning into his familiar if contrived posture as a far-right culture warrior provocateur. This week, DeSantis banned rainbow lights on Florida bridges ahead of Gay Pride Month and deleted the phrase \xe2\x80\x9cclimate change\xe2\x80\x9d from Florida law. The legislation also bans offshore wind turbines, protects endangered gas stoves , frees state agencies from the tyranny of fuel-efficient vehicles, and so on. Pure MAGA red meat.

More ambitiously, DeSantis is also strategically preparing to throw the weight of his remaining political capital behind Florida\xe2\x80\x99s highly controversial six-week abortion ban, which he signed into law in April, and now faces a ballot initiative in November that would amend the state constitution to guarantee broader abortion access. It is, of course, a high-risk strategy, considering that nearly 60 percent of registered voters in Florida support the ballot measure\xe2\x80\x94the same margin that would be needed for it to pass into law. It also positions DeSantis to the right of Trump, a flanking maneuver that failed him during the Republican primary. Trump, who said he\xe2\x80\x99s leaving abortion up to the states, initially called the Florida ban \xe2\x80\x9ctoo harsh.\xe2\x80\x9d

So far, DeSantis has been relatively quiet about the looming abortion battle. But sources tell me that he\xe2\x80\x99s building out a campaign to defeat the effort and will spend as much as $50 million on it. \xe2\x80\x9cHe wants to be able to make the point that in his state the ballot initiative failed, unlike Ohio or Kansas,\xe2\x80\x9d said a source familiar with the governor\xe2\x80\x99s thinking. \xe2\x80\x9cHe wants to be able to say that Florida is redder than Texas.\xe2\x80\x9d
\xe2\x80\x9928 Maneuvering
Abortion bans remain unpopular nationally, even among Republican voters. But DeSantis is said to believe that the presidential primary landscape will be much different in 2028 without Trump on the ballot. If he can come out of the gate with something like a six-week abortion ban on his r\xc3\xa9sum\xc3\xa9, it might position him to win, once again, the key endorsement of evangelical leader Bob Vander Plaats in Iowa, setting him up favorably in early state voting.

Vander Plaats doesn\xe2\x80\x99t seem to totally disagree. \xe2\x80\x9cSometimes the right thing to do matches up with good politics,\xe2\x80\x9d he told me. \xe2\x80\x9cI have not been coaching him or advising. I did discuss it early on in regards to the referendum. I know that his heart will be to defeat it. As far as using it to launch his 2028 campaign, he\xe2\x80\x99s got a lot of things that he\xe2\x80\x99s done right. This would be another one in a long list of things.\xe2\x80\x9d

Just as importantly, perhaps, it\xe2\x80\x99s an opportunity for DeSantis to raise heaps of cash that he can sock away for 2028. He still has control over a massive budget as governor, which allows him to fly around the country for fundraisers and media appearances, and provides him the capability of raising unlimited sums of cash through Florida political committees. State law also allows for the balance to be transferred to super PACs. Last year, of course, DeSantis transferred $82.5 million from his 2022 reelection campaign to support his presidential run.

\xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s not so much that his star has fallen, his fave/unfavorable is the same as it\xe2\x80\x99s always been,\xe2\x80\x9d said Republican David Jolly , the former Florida congressman. \xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s that he\xe2\x80\x99s become irrelevant to the national conversation. He needs a high-intensity issue, and there\xe2\x80\x99s none more intense than abortion.\xe2\x80\x9d
Convention Wisdom
As a result of the Shell Bay Club truce, I\xe2\x80\x99m told, DeSantis agreed to raise money for Trump, and Trump said he would consider giving DeSantis a coveted primetime speaking slot at the Republican National Convention. Typically, these spots are reserved for surrogates who can energize a specific constituency, like Black or Hispanic voters, although they may also be offered to runners-up as a sign of party unity. Getting that nod would be a major coup for DeSantis, especially given the beef between Trump\xe2\x80\x99s team and his own. Trump campaign advisor Susie Wiles , who defected from DeSantis, really doesn\xe2\x80\x99t care for her former boss.

DeSantis, for one, is keeping up his end of the bargain while managing his own relationships. According to a seemingly strategically placed leak , DeSantis hosted a fundraiser for the pro-Trump super PAC Right for America, which is supported by his ally and donor Ike Perlmutter , the Disney scourge. (These DeSantis donors were wary of putting money into Trump\xe2\x80\x99s primary super PAC, MAGA Inc., out of concern that the entity is paying for many of his legal bills.) Sealing the deal, Trump called in to the event and made a point to announce over speakerphone, \xe2\x80\x9cRon, I love that you\xe2\x80\x99re back.\xe2\x80\x9d DeSantis is also slated to host fundraisers for Trump in South Florida and Texas. \xe2\x80\x9cThere\xe2\x80\x99s at least a d\xc3\xa9tente,\xe2\x80\x9d remarked a Florida insider. \xe2\x80\x9cEveryone will tell you that the only thing Trump cares about is winning. He\xe2\x80\x99s willing to put some things aside.\xe2\x80\x9d

Meanwhile, DeSantis is keeping the engine warm for his future political revival. He hosted a donor thank-you event in Fort Lauderdale two weeks ago. Indeed, there are lots of DeSantis donors who have little interest in donating to Trump, but they do business in Florida and want to know that DeSantis can work with him. \xe2\x80\x9cHe\xe2\x80\x99s been cognizant of mining his donor network,\xe2\x80\x9d noted the Florida insider. However, bigger names like Ken Griffin are unlikely to give to Trump even at DeSantis\xe2\x80\x99s behest.

As for the prospect that Casey DeSantis , the media-savvy first lady who polls high in ranked-choice voting scenarios, will run for governor when her husband\xe2\x80\x99s term is up in 2026, Florida insiders tell me that she\xe2\x80\x99s still \xe2\x80\x9ctraumatized\xe2\x80\x9d by attacks on the campaign trail\xe2\x80\x94at least for the moment. Not so long ago, Casey was the object of endless speculation that she might continue the DeSantis political dynasty in Florida, perhaps as a sort of placeholder until the couple move into the White House in 2028. \xe2\x80\x9cShe took it on the chin like DeSantis,\xe2\x80\x9d said one Florida pollster. \xe2\x80\x9cHer agenda is tied to Ron\xe2\x80\x99s culture wars, but she\xe2\x80\x99s polling ahead of Matt Gaetz , Wilton Simpson , and Ashley Moody by double digits. I wouldn\xe2\x80\x99t be surprised if she\xe2\x80\x99s a candidate one day. She wins a four- or five-way race where you don\xe2\x80\x99t have to have 50 percent.\xe2\x80\x9d

There are hard choices to be made for the DeSantii, for whom being in office has become a lifestyle. DeSantis had a net worth of $300,000 when he was elected governor; now he has a $300,000 golf simulator in the governor\xe2\x80\x99s mansion and a lot more money in the bank, thanks to sales of his bestselling 2023 autobiography. He uses the state-owned, $15 million Cessna Citation so often that local reporters have dubbed it \xe2\x80\x9cAir DeSantis.\xe2\x80\x9d With friends like these, a trip through the private sector will serve him well.
FOUR STORIES WE\xe2\x80\x99RE TALKING ABOUT
Lewis\xe2\x80\x99s WaPo Manifesto
Walking through the C.E.O.\xe2\x80\x99s newly unveiled plan.
DYLAN BYERS
Bieber\xe2\x80\x99s Blush War
Foreshadowing Rhode\xe2\x80\x99s looming\xc2\xa0battle with Rare Beauty.
RACHEL STRUGATZ
The Lionsgate Den
Detailing an only-in-Hollywood micro-scandal.
WILLIAM D. COHAN
Johnson\xe2\x80\x99s Israel Pincer
Unpacking the House speaker\xe2\x80\x99s pro-Israel crusade.
TINA NGUYEN
